name: Money Coach at Behavioral Cents, LLC
description_short: Financial therapist offering free and paid behavioral coaching on debt, overshopping, and couples money issues, founded by Carrie Rattle with 30 years of financial services experience.
description_long:
Behavioral Cents, LLC is a financial coaching practice founded and led by Carrie Rattle, a Financial Therapist with an MBA and CDFA designation. The company operates as a boutique advisory firm focused on the psychological and behavioral aspects of personal finance rather than transactional financial products. Rattle brings 30 years of experience from the banking and financial services industry, combined with specialized training in financial therapy, couples counseling around money, and compulsive shopping disorders.

The company offers several core services: Debt FREE coaching (debt management and strategy), Stopping Overshopping™ (addressing compulsive shopping behaviors), and Partners in Love & Money (couples financial counseling). They provide expert financial coaching tailored to individual client situations. The firm also offers a free quiz to assess wealth potential, suggesting a freemium model where initial assessment is free but ongoing coaching is fee-based. Services are delivered through one-on-one coaching relationships and appear to include both individual and couples sessions.

Behavioral Cents differentiates itself through founder Carrie Rattle's specialized credentials and training. She completed 18 months of clinical training with Dr. April Benson, PhD, a nationally recognized compulsive shopping specialist, and trained with Olivia Mellan, a critically acclaimed psychotherapist specializing in couples and money dynamics. The company frames financial coaching through a behavioral and therapeutic lens rather than purely technical financial planning. The founder's mission emphasizes financial equity for women and positions money self-care as essential alongside physical and mental health.

The primary limitation is that Behavioral Cents appears to be a solo practitioner or very small firm (the website centers entirely on founder Carrie Rattle), which may limit scalability and accessibility. Pricing for paid services is not disclosed on the website. While the free-help category captures their free initial assessment offering, the core business model relies on paid coaching fees rather than non-profit credit counseling typical of NFCC-certified agencies. Clients seeking completely free ongoing assistance should clarify pricing before engaging.
pros: Founder has 30 years of direct financial services industry experience in banking and credit
Specialized training in compulsive shopping and overshopping with clinical expert Dr. April Benson, PhD
Credentials in financial therapy and couples counseling (trained by Olivia Mellan)
Offers free initial wealth potential quiz to assess financial situation
Addresses psychological and behavioral components of financial problems, not just technical solutions
Provides couples-focused money coaching to address trust and communication breakdowns
Founder holds multiple relevant credentials (Financial Therapist, MBA, CDFA, AFC)
Mission explicitly focused on financial equity and women's financial empowerment
cons: Appears to be a solo practitioner business with limited scalability and availability
No pricing information disclosed on website; may be expensive for budget-constrained consumers
While offering free assessment, ongoing coaching is fee-based rather than free like NFCC credit counseling
Limited online presence of reviews or third-party verification of coaching effectiveness
Not a non-profit; cannot offer the free ongoing counseling typical of traditional credit counseling agencies
best_for: Individuals struggling with compulsive shopping or overshopping behaviors seeking therapeutic intervention
Couples with money conflicts, trust issues, or power imbalances around financial decisions
Professionals and women earning good income but lacking confidence in financial decision-making
People with debt who want behavioral coaching alongside a strategic payoff plan
diagnosis: Behavioral Cents is best for individuals and couples seeking therapeutic, psychology-informed financial coaching rather than transactional financial products or free non-profit credit counseling. The main caveat is that this is a paid coaching service (after free initial assessment) from a solo practitioner, so availability may be limited and costs are not publicly disclosed—clients should confirm pricing and availability align with their needs before committing.
services: Couples financial counseling (Partners in Love & Money program)
Financial therapy addressing psychological money behaviors
Expert thought partnership on complex money decisions
